body: PT Transcoal Pacific Tbk (TCPI) plans to add 12 new fleets by 2025, which is expected to boost revenue performance, with a capital expenditure of IDR 700 billion, a 55.55% increase from 2024. The company is optimistic that the new fleets will increase nickel ore transportation volume by 68.75% to 1.35 million metric tons in 2025, up from an estimated 800,000 metric tons in 2024.
title: PT Transcoal Pacific to Add 12 New Fleet Units by 2025 to Boost Revenue Performance

body: Pt Sejahtera Raya Perkasa (SRP), a shareholder of PT Indomobil Sukses Internasional Tbk (IMAS), sold 55 million IMAS shares at IDR 652 per share, 73.3% lower than the closing price of IDR 1,130 per share on November 7, 2024, reducing its ownership stake to 8.72% or 348.56 million shares. Despite a 1.01% increase in IMAS share price to IDR 1,000 per share, the company's share price has fallen 31.03% or 450 points since the beginning of the year.
title: PT Sejahtera Raya Perkasa sells 55 million IMAS shares at 73.3% discount to closing price.

body: Hapsoro's portion in PT Sanurhasta Mitra Tbk (Mina) increased by 51.44% after acquiring an additional 3.71% stake, with a total of 243.75 million shares purchased at IDR 23 per share, or IDR 5.6 billion. As a result, Hapsoro now holds a 47.72% stake in Mina, with a total of 3.01 billion shares, up from 45.71% previously held by Bup.
title: Hapsoro's stake in PT Mina increases to 47.72% after acquiring additional 3.71% shares.

body: PT Cipta Mapan Logistik (CML) has secured a IDR 40 billion financing facility from its affiliated company, PT Taruna Bina Sarana, with an 8.5% interest rate per annum and a 6-year tenure, to boost its working capital. The company is targeting 10-12% revenue growth this year, supported by a IDR 500 billion capital expenditure plan, which will be allocated towards warehouse construction, landbank acquisition, and improving sustainability.
title: PT Cipta Mapan Logistik secures IDR 40 billion financing facility to boost working capital.
